title: Code Points, Grapheme Clusters, Character Categories, and Labels · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers code points, grapheme clusters, character categories, and labels into a changed honestly labeled text-metrics panel. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of honestly labeled text-metrics panel with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Distinguish bytes, code points, extended grapheme clusters, display cells, letters, and stakeholder-defined characters.
Use Unicode categories or explicit predicates to separate letters, marks, numbers, punctuation, symbols, separators, and controls.
State whether character frequencies operate on original or normalized text, preserve case, and include or exclude marks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Codepoint Grapheme
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 9769 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to label and reconcile code-point, grapheme, and letter metrics without overstating browser segmentation; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish bytes, code points, extended grapheme clusters, display cells, letters, and stakeholder-defined characters.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Codepoint Grapheme.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Metric Label Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-metric-label-evidence-1bggxi" that uses case 1539 to label and reconcile code-point, grapheme, and letter metrics without overstating browser segmentation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Metric Label Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Metric Label Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Metric Label Evidence, keep Metric Label Evidence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-metric-label-evidence-1bggxi
# Competency: bookbot-metric-label-evidence.
# Case 1539: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_metric_label_evidence_1bggxi(code_points: int = 14, graphemes: int = 11, letters: int = 9, labels_explicit: bool = True, segmentation_transfer_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1bggxi = "1bggxi-ixggb1-1539"
    assert evidence_variant_1bggxi
    evidence_axes_1bggxi = (
        "corpus1bggxi",
        "policyixggb1",
        "revision1539",
        "command1bggxi1539",
        "reportixggb11539",
        "test15391bggxi",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1bggxi)) == 6
    if min(code_points, graphemes, letters) < 0:
        return False, code_points, "metric-count"
    if graphemes > code_points or letters > code_points:
        return False, graphemes, "unit-reconciliation"
    if not labels_explicit:
        return False, letters, "ambiguous-unit"
    if not segmentation_transfer_named:
        return False, graphemes, "grapheme-overclaim"
    assert letters <= code_points
    return True, graphemes, "honest-text-metrics"
